fr:mainmatter.fr:p: This follows in essentially the same way as sections need not be embeddings. Let \mathcal {K} be \mathbf {Cat} and consider the circle S^1 viewed as the free groupoid generated by a point and a loop. Then the base point \mathbf {1} \xrightarrow { \mathsf { base } } S^1 is not fully faithful, as there are \mathbb {Z} many endomorphisms on \mathsf { base } that do not lie in the image of \mathbf {1} \xrightarrow { \mathsf { base } } S^1.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Points in a 2-category need not be full

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {K} be a 2-category with a terminal object \mathbf {1}; a 1-cell \mathbf {1} \xrightarrow {p}E in \mathcal {K} need not be full.

This follows in essentially the same way as sections need not be embeddings. Let \mathcal {K} be \mathbf {Cat} and consider the circle S^1 viewed as the free groupoid generated by a point and a loop. Then the base point \mathbf {1} \xrightarrow { \mathsf { base } } S^1 is not fully faithful, as there are \mathbb {Z} many endomorphisms on \mathsf { base } that do not lie in the image of \mathbf {1} \xrightarrow { \mathsf { base } } S^1.

Although it is the case that a section in a 1-category is always a monomorphism, it is not the case that every section in an (∞,1)-category is an embedding. This can be seen in homotopy type theory, by considering the splitting \mathbf {1} \xrightarrow { \lambda \_ . \mathsf { base } }S^1 \xrightarrow {!} \mathbf {1}. If \lambda \_ . \mathsf { base } were an embedding, it would mean that its fibers were propositions: but the fiber of \lambda \_ . \mathsf { base } over \mathsf { base } is the loop space of S^1, which is a proper set (the integers).
